---
title: "Dictionary Index"
---

<div class="claim-boundary-strip">
<span class="claim-boundary-label">Claim boundary</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-executable">definitions and guardrails</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-proved">proofs live in theorem ids</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-speculative">terms do not prove claims</span>
</div>

The dictionary fixes the meanings of Circle Calculus terms so papers, Lean declarations, Python reference models, and widgets do not drift. The generated index links each entry back to theorem, paper, widget, and glyph usage where the manifests expose those relationships.

Click an id to open a filtered, shareable view of the relevant dictionary, theorem, paper, target, or glyph index.

<div class="warning-box">
Dictionary entries are definitions and guardrails. They are not theorem proofs.
</div>

::: {.learning-unit}

## How To Use The Dictionary While Reading

When a lesson introduces a term, read the dictionary card before opening the theorem card. The dictionary tells you what the term means in this project, what it does not mean yet, and where the same term is reused across papers, widgets, and proofs.

For the first pass through S1, follow this vocabulary ladder:

```text
circle -> node -> rotation -> coil -> period -> winding
```

:::

<div class="dictionary-index" data-dictionary-index="all"></div>

## Core S1 Entries

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0001"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0002"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0101"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0201"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0205"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0301"></div>

<script type="module" src="widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
